title: Why your literature review is probably incomplete without AI
post:
Here's an uncomfortable truth: human memory and keyword-based search are fundamentally limited ways to survey a field. We miss papers. We forget connections. We anchor on what we know.

AI can process thousands of papers in hours, surface patterns across decades of research, and identify gaps that even domain experts overlook. That's not a knock on researchers — it's just math. The volume of published work has exploded beyond what any individual can reasonably consume.

The question isn't whether to use AI in your literature review. It's whether you can afford not to.

What's your approach to staying comprehensive in your field? Thoughts?
